title: moraea viscaria var. bituminosa
intro: Moraea viscaria var. bituminosa, also known as the sticky moraea or sticky yellow iris, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Iridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Moraea viscaria var. bituminosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal teas, or it can be used to make a poultice or salve for topical application.
